question_text: The gravitational force between two objects depends on their masses and the distance between them. If the distance between Earth and the Moon were to double, what would happen to the gravitational force between them?

options.label: B
options.text: It would be reduced to one quarter of its original value

options.label: C
options.text: It would be reduced to half of its original value

correct_answer: It would be reduced to one quarter of its original value
explanation: Gravitational force follows an inverse square law, meaning it is inversely proportional to the square of the distance between two objects (F ∝ 1/d²). When the distance doubles, the force is divided by 2² = 4, so it becomes 1/4 of its original strength.
